summary: Matched more of sysdolphin baselib fobj by replacing several hand-written/remaining assembly routines with C in src/sysdolphin/baselib/fobj.c, including parseFloat, func_8036ADDC, func_8036AE38, func_8036AE70, and the previously NON_MATCHING HSD_FObjReqAnimAll path. The PR also added a typed splGetHelmite prototype and adjusted remaining fobj.s references to SDA2 constants after moving constants/code into C.

key_files.role: Main C matching work; removed NON_MATCHING/inline asm fallback for HSD_FObjReqAnimAll and added C implementations for fobj helper routines.
Large assembly deletion for newly matched routines; remaining HSD_FObjInterpretAnim assembly retained with adjusted SDA2 label references.
Replaced unknown splGetHelmite declaration with f32 splGetHelmite(f32, f32, f32, f32, f32, f32).

what_changed: Converted HSD_FObjReqAnim to an inline C helper and removed the old NON_MATCHING conditional plus inline assembly implementation of HSD_FObjReqAnimAll.
Added C implementation of parseFloat(u8** pos, u8 arg1), handling raw 32-bit float reads when arg1 is zero and scaled signed/unsigned 1-byte or 2-byte fixed-point encodings selected by arg1 & 0xE0.
Added C implementation of func_8036ADDC(u8** pos), decoding a variable-length byte sequence using the high bit as continuation and accumulating 7-bit chunks after an initial 3-bit field.
Added C implementation of func_8036AE38(HSD_FObj* fobj), moving a pending op into op_intrp when flag 0x40 is set, clearing 0x40, setting 0x80, and copying p1 to p0.

Added C implementation of func_8036AE70(HSD_FObj* fobj, void* arg1, void (*arg2)(void*, s32, f32*)), calculating an animation value based on op_intrp cases and invoking the object update callback with obj_type and a stack f32.
Added a concrete prototype for splGetHelmite so C fobj interpolation code can call it with six f32 arguments.
Removed local sdata2 definitions for lbl_804DE4D0, lbl_804DE4D8, lbl_804DE4E0, and lbl_804DE4E8 from fobj.s, leaving lbl_804DE4F0.
Added .set entries for _SDA2_BASE_, lbl_804DE4D0, and lbl_804DE4E0 in fobj.s and rewrote some remaining HSD_FObjInterpretAnim loads to use label-_SDA2_BASE_(r2).
smart_moves: Inlining HSD_FObjReqAnim let HSD_FObjReqAnimAll share the reset logic directly in C, replacing an asm fallback that existed because the startframe integer-to-float conversion had not previously matched.
The parseFloat implementation mirrors the assembly's encoding dispatch with arg1 & 0xE0 and denom = 1 << (arg1 & 0x1F), making the packed animation-data format explicit and reusable.

The func_8036AE70 implementation preserves callback behavior by computing the result into a local f32 and passing its address, matching the assembly pattern that stores to stack then calls through a function pointer.
Adding the splGetHelmite prototype avoided keeping the Hermite interpolation call opaque, enabling typed C code for op_intrp cases 3, 4, and 5.

Keeping HSD_FObjInterpretAnim in assembly while matching adjacent helpers limited scope: the PR removed hundreds of asm lines without forcing the largest remaining interpreter routine to match at the same time.
decomp_lessons: When a nonmatching wrapper exists only because of a numeric conversion, try making the inner helper inline; this can force the compiler to emit the same conversion sequence in the caller.

Packed animation byte streams often encode type and scale in one byte; preserve exact signedness for each case, such as signed 8-bit, unsigned 8-bit, signed 16-bit assembled from little-endian bytes, and unsigned 16-bit.

For variable-length integer decoders, model the continuation bit and shift progression explicitly rather than over-abstracting; here the first byte contributes a 3-bit amount and later bytes contribute 7-bit groups.

For fobj interpolation code, HSD_FObj fields observed in the diff include flags, op, op_intrp, time, fterm, p0, p1, d0, d1, obj_type, ad, ad_head, startframe, and next; preserving these field-level side effects is central to matching.
Partial asm retention may require explicit SDA2 label plumbing if constants move from local assembly data into C-generated data or another compilation unit.
naming_conventions: Existing exported HSD names use HSD_FObj* capitalization, such as HSD_FObjGetState, HSD_FObjReqAnim, HSD_FObjReqAnimAll, HSD_FObjStopAnim, HSD_FObjStopAnimAll, and HSD_FObjInterpretAnim.
Unknown helper functions remained address-style names where no semantic name was evidenced: func_8036ADDC, func_8036AE38, and func_8036AE70.
parseFloat used a semantic camelCase name already present as an assembly global.
Spline helper naming in this area uses spl-prefixed camelCase, evidenced by splGetHelmite and splArcLengthPoint.
The diff spells the function as splGetHelmite, not the usual English spelling Hermite; future work should preserve the existing symbol spelling unless separate evidence supports renaming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed assembly for parseFloat, func_8036ADDC, func_8036AE38, func_8036AE70, and HSD_FObjReqAnimAll after matching them in C.
Used an inline C helper for HSD_FObjReqAnim to affect caller code generation in HSD_FObjReqAnimAll.
Used pointer post-increment patterns like ((*pos)++)[0] and *(*pos)++ to match byte-stream reads and pointer advancement.
Used bit masks matching assembly behavior: arg1 & 0x1F, arg1 & 0xE0, val & 0x80, val & 0x7F, fobj->flags & 0x40, flags clearing with ~0x40/~0x80-equivalent masks, and flag setting with 0x80.
Preserved stack-local callback argument behavior in func_8036AE70 by storing the computed f32 in sp14 and passing &sp14 to arg2.
For remaining assembly, replaced some @sda21 references with explicit label-_SDA2_BASE_(r2) after adding .set _SDA2_BASE_, .set lbl_804DE4D0, and .set lbl_804DE4E0.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename or document func_8036ADDC, func_8036AE38, or func_8036AE70.
Search subsequent fobj PRs for matching HSD_FObjInterpretAnim and cleanup of remaining fobj.s assembly.
Check later header organization for whether splGetHelmite moved out of include/functions.h into a dedicated spline header.
Investigate whether splGetHelmite spelling is confirmed by symbols or should remain as-is for matching.
